element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic traversal and interaction with NPCs
World map traversal via Globulator; must be summoned to block exits
Gathering clues, identifying artifacts, locating Koopas
Returning artifacts to correct tourist information centers; answering verification questions
Summoning Yoshi to consume Pokey and unlock progression
Sequential boss defeats to progress through castle floors

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final confrontation after all Koopalings defeated

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully identifying correct city via NPC dialogue
Locating and defeating Koopa to obtain artifact
Returning artifact to correct information center and passing authentication
Summoning Yoshi to clear Pokey blockade
typical_tension_triggers: Arrival in unfamiliar city without immediate context
Multiple Koopas present in city requiring defeat
Uncertainty about artifact location and authenticity requirements
Pokey blocking exit, requiring Yoshi retrieval
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Educational cycle: exploration and information gathering (low tension) followed by combat encounters (moderate tension), repeating across five distinct city visits before escalating to boss sequences (high tension)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple tourist information centers and NPCs within each city allow varied dialogue paths and clue discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction at Bowser's castle hub; first city acts as tutorial for NPC dialogue and basic artifact retrieval mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate tension as player manages multiple Koopas per city, authenticates artifacts, and coordinates Yoshi across world map; tension increases through progressive city visits
difficulty_curve.late_game: High tension: sequential Koopalings (mid-late) followed by final Bowser confrontation; multiple failure points increase challenge
notable_spikes: First Koopalings appearance after fifth city completion
Final Bowser boss fight with different mechanics between NES/SNES versions

notable_level_patterns.description: Each city follows: arrival/identification → NPC interrogation for clues → artifact location discovery → Koopa defeat(s) → artifact return and authentication → Pokey blockade removal via Yoshi → progression to next city
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Cairo, New York City, Paris (any of five main cities)

notable_level_patterns.description: Top-level spatial abstraction where player moves Yoshi across world map to reunite with Luigi, representing overworld traversal between PORTALS-accessible cities
After five cities, linear floor-by-floor advancement through Bowser's castle with mandatory Koopalings encounters, culminating in final chamber for Bowser fight

sources_used: wikipedia - Mario Is Missing! (gameplay mechanics, progression structure, city locations, boss encounters)
tcrf - Mario is Missing! (unused graphics and design artifacts indicating scrapped mechanics)
